description: Core malloc/free internals rules for heap exploitation reasoning

behavior.on_free: If tcache for this size has < 7 entries, goes to tcache. Otherwise goes to fastbin or unsorted bin.
behavior.on_malloc: First checks tcache for matching size. If found, returns immediately without coalescing.

safe_linking.notes: Only applies to tcache and fastbin FD pointers

exploitation_notes: To force chunk into unsorted bin: fill tcache (7 frees) then free one more
Alternative: fake chunk size > 1040 to bypass tcache entirely
Tcache does NOT consolidate with adjacent chunks

unsorted_bin.threshold: Chunks > tcache_max_size (1040) OR tcache full for that size

behavior.on_free: Chunk is placed in unsorted bin list. FD and BK both point to main_arena.
behavior.on_malloc: Unsorted bin is searched first. If exact match, removed. If larger, split and remainder goes back to unsorted bin.
behavior.fd_bk_value: Both point to main_arena + 96 (unsorted bin offset)

exploitation_notes: Unsorted bin leak is most reliable libc leak method
FD and BK both contain libc addresses
Reading freed chunk (UAF) reveals these pointers
Chunk must NOT be in tcache for this to work

fastbin.size_range: 32 to 128 (before 2.26: up to 128, after: up to 128 or tcache handles it)

behavior.on_free: If size <= 128 and tcache full, goes to fastbin. LIFO order.
behavior.on_malloc: Fastbin checked after tcache. Exact size match only.
behavior.no_consolidation: Fastbin chunks are NOT consolidated with neighbors

exploitation_notes: Fastbin attack: overwrite FD to allocate at arbitrary address
Size check: malloc checks if returned chunk size matches request (fastbin check)
Double-free detection: checks if FD points to same chunk (before 2.29)

behavior.on_free: If size > fastbin_max and <= 1008, goes to small bin.
If size > 1008, goes to large bin.
behavior.sorted_by_size: Chunks sorted by size within bin

prev_inuse_check.description: When freeing chunk P, glibc checks prev_inuse bit of NEXT chunk
prev_inuse_check.exploitation_implication: Must set prev_inuse=1 on chunk after fake chunk to avoid crash

consolidation_check.description: If prev_inuse of chunk P is 0, glibc consolidates with previous chunk
consolidation_check.code: if (!prev_inuse(p)) { prevsize = prev_size(p); size += prevsize; p = chunk_at_offset(p, -prevsize); if (chunksize(p) != prevsize) malloc_printerr("corrupted size vs. prev_size"); unlink_chunk(av, p); }
consolidation_check.exploitation_implication: If creating fake chunk with prev_inuse=0, must also fake prev_size of next chunk to match

size_check.description: Chunk size must be >= MINSIZE (32) and aligned to MALLOC_ALIGNMENT (16)
size_check.exploitation_implication: Fake chunk size must be valid (>= 32, aligned to 16)

next_size_check.description: Next chunk's size must be valid and within arena bounds
next_size_check.exploitation_implication: Fake chunk must have valid next chunk size

fastbin_size_check.description: When allocating from fastbin, returned chunk size must match request
fastbin_size_check.exploitation_implication: When using fastbin attack, target address - 16 must have valid size matching fastbin index

tcache_double_free_check.description: Tcache now checks for double-free by walking the list
tcache_double_free_check.exploitation_implication: Double-free into tcache is detected in glibc >= 2.34

minimum_requirements.prev_inuse: Must be 1 (PREV_INUSE bit set) to avoid consolidation
minimum_requirements.next_chunk_size: Must be valid size to pass next_size_check

for_unsorted_bin.size: Must be > 1040 (tcache max) to bypass tcache
additional_requirements: prev_inuse of next chunk must be 1
prev_size of next chunk must match if prev_inuse=0
Must not overlap with existing allocated chunks

for_fastbin_attack.target_address: target - 16 must have valid size matching fastbin index
additional_requirements: FD pointer must be valid or NULL
Must pass fastbin size check on allocation

allocated.prev_size: 8 bytes (if prev chunk free, otherwise part of prev chunk's data)

freed.bk: 8 bytes (backward pointer, only for unsorted/small/large bins)

metadata_bits.PREV_INUSE: Bit 0 of size field. 1 = previous chunk is allocated.
metadata_bits.IS_MMAPPED: Bit 1 of size field. 1 = chunk was obtained via mmap.
metadata_bits.NON_MAIN_ARENA: Bit 2 of size field. 1 = chunk belongs to non-main arena (thread cache).

version_specific_changes.2.32: Introduced safe linking for tcache/fastbin FD pointers
version_specific_changes.2.34: tcache double-free strengthened, hooks deprecated, malloc_consolidate on large scanf
version_specific_changes.2.35: more tcache hardening, environ leak still viable

malloc_consolidate_trigger.description: Large input to scanf (>0x400 bytes) triggers malloc_consolidate, moving tcache chunks to unsorted bin
malloc_consolidate_trigger.use_case: Force tcache chunks into unsorted bin for libc leak without filling tcache manually
malloc_consolidate_trigger.impact: HIGH - alternative to tcache fill for libc leak

environ_stack_leak.description: Use libc's environ variable to leak stack address
environ_stack_leak.mechanism: environ points to stack environment pointer, which points to stack frame
environ_stack_leak.use_case: Get stack address for ROP chain placement

index_confusion_uaf.description: Index variable mismatch creates UAF vulnerability
index_confusion_uaf.mechanism: chunk_index_a controls which pointer is NULLed, chunk_index_b controls which is freed. If they differ, freed chunk remains accessible.
index_confusion_uaf.exploitation: Overflow index counter to wrap around, causing mismatch between free and NULL operations
index_confusion_uaf.impact: HIGH - creates UAF from index confusion

single_byte_to_large_overflow.description: Single byte overflow corrupts chunk size LSB, enabling larger overflow
single_byte_to_large_overflow.mechanism: Overwrite LSB of next chunk's size (e.g., 0x100 -> 0x180), free it, then allocate larger chunk to get overflow
single_byte_to_large_overflow.impact: HIGH - escalates single byte to arbitrary overflow

calloc_no_tcache.description: calloc does NOT use tcache, always gets from fastbin/unsorted bin
calloc_no_tcache.mechanism: calloc bypasses tcache entirely, useful for getting chunks from specific bins
calloc_no_tcache.use_case: Get chunk from fastbin when tcache would normally service it
calloc_no_tcache.impact: MEDIUM - useful for specific bin manipulation

heap_key_leak_safe_linking.description: Leak heap key (XOR mask) from tcache FD pointer
heap_key_leak_safe_linking.mechanism: When chunk freed to tcache, FD = real_addr ^ (chunk_addr >> 12). If FD points to NULL, encoded value reveals upper bits of chunk address.
